jokes.text: Nine years. Seems like a long time, doesn't it? It is.
jokes.setup: Reflective opening about nine years seeming long
jokes.punchline: The immediate, matter-of-fact confirmation 'It is'
jokes.explanation: Classic Jerry deadpan delivery - sets up expectation of profound reflection then immediately deflates it with blunt honesty

jokes.text: Except for summer... where nothing seemed to happen for months at a time.
jokes.setup: Talking about packed experiences over nine years
jokes.punchline: Meta joke about TV show hiatus periods
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-commentary on television production breaks disguised as character reflection - very sophisticated

jokes.text: Do you ever get the feeling like you had a haircut, but you didn't have one?
jokes.setup: Random observation about phantom haircut feeling
jokes.punchline: The absurdity of the non-existent sensation
jokes.explanation: Perfectly captures George's neurotic overthinking of mundane sensations - very character-specific absurdism

jokes.text: What kind of lives are these? We're like children. We're not men. No, we're not. We're not men. We're pathetic, you know that? Like I don't know that I'm pathetic.
jokes.setup: George having existential crisis about their maturity
jokes.punchline: The escalating self-awareness of his own patheticness
jokes.explanation: Perfect George neurotic spiral - the repetitive structure mirrors his obsessive thought patterns

jokes.text: I was in the pool!
jokes.setup: Apparent shrinkage embarrassment situation
jokes.punchline: The desperate, famous explanation
jokes.explanation: Iconic callback line - perfect economy, became cultural catchphrase, quintessentially George's mortification

jokes.text: Since when do you smoke? I've always smoked. I've never seen you smoke. Oh, yeah, well. Big smoker.
jokes.setup: Jerry questioning when Kramer started smoking
jokes.punchline: Kramer's ridiculous claim to always being a 'big smoker'
jokes.explanation: Classic Kramer absurdist logic - retrofitting his history to fit current behavior

jokes.text: That's why I'm different. I can sense the slightest human suffering. Are you sensing anything right now?
jokes.setup: Kramer claims supernatural empathy abilities
jokes.punchline: Jerry's sarcastic test of the claim
jokes.explanation: Great setup-punchline with Jerry as perfect straight man deflating Kramer's grandiose self-image

jokes.text: Do you have any conceivable reason for even getting up in the morning? I like to get the Daily News.
jokes.setup: Existential question about George's purpose in life
jokes.punchline: George's pathetically mundane reason for living
jokes.explanation: Perfectly captures George's small-stakes existence - the contrast between profound question and trivial answer is brilliant

jokes.text: Every group has someone that they all make fun of. Like us, with Elaine.
jokes.setup: Universal truth about group dynamics
jokes.punchline: Casually identifying Elaine as their group's target
jokes.explanation: Brutally honest group dynamics observation delivered with casual cruelty

jokes.text: You're bald! No, I'm not. I was bald.
jokes.setup: Elaine stating the obvious about George
jokes.punchline: George's ridiculous distinction between present and past baldness
jokes.explanation: Classic George denial logic - the absurd grammatical distinction shows his desperate avoidance of reality

jokes.text: Stella!
jokes.setup: Kramer dramatically calling out
jokes.punchline: Classic Streetcar Named Desire reference
jokes.explanation: Standard pop culture reference - fits Kramer's theatrical nature but not particularly original

jokes.text: Now, don't you two see that you're in love with each other?
jokes.setup: George trying to mediate a fight
jokes.punchline: His ridiculous romantic assessment of the situation
jokes.explanation: George's misguided attempt at relationship wisdom - mildly funny but predictable

jokes.text: Kramer goes to a fantasy camp. His whole life is a fantasy camp. People should plunk down $2000 to live like him for a week. Do nothing. Fall ass-backwards into money. Mooch food off your neighbors... and have sex without dating. That's a fantasy camp.
jokes.setup: Discussion of fantasy camps
jokes.punchline: Jerry's detailed breakdown of why Kramer's entire existence is the ultimate fantasy
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character analysis disguised as observational comedy - perfectly captures Kramer's impossible lifestyle

jokes.text: No boxers, no Jockeys. The only thing between him and us is a thin layer of gabardine.
jokes.setup: Discussion of underwear choices
jokes.punchline: The horrifying realization about Kramer's commando situation
jokes.explanation: Great escalation from underwear choice to existential horror - 'gabardine' is perfect word choice

jokes.text: I'm out there, Jerry, and I'm loving every minute of it.
jokes.setup: Kramer discussing his underwear-free lifestyle
jokes.punchline: His enthusiastic embrace of the situation
jokes.explanation: Pure Kramer confidence in an objectively uncomfortable situation - his joy makes it funnier

jokes.text: Yeah, and you're an anti-dentite. I am not an anti-dentite. You're a rabid anti-dentite.
jokes.setup: Jerry's prejudice against dentists
jokes.punchline: The absurd term 'anti-dentite' and escalation to 'rabid'
jokes.explanation: Brilliant neologism that became part of cultural lexicon - perfect parody of discrimination terminology

jokes.text: I think I'm pretty much like you. Only successful.
jokes.setup: Whatley comparing himself to Jerry
jokes.punchline: The brutal qualifier about success
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy in the devastating comparison - the pause before 'Only successful' is key

jokes.text: Give me your home number and I'll call you later. Well, I'm sorry, we're not allowed to do that. I guess you don't want people calling you at home. No. Well, now you know how I feel.
jokes.setup: Telemarketer won't give home number
jokes.punchline: Jerry's perfect logical trap about unwanted calls
jokes.explanation: Brilliant logical reversal that became a template for dealing with telemarketers - perfect structure and earned payoff

jokes.text: Jerry. Kill me. Kill me now. I'm begging you.
jokes.setup: George's sleep-deprived desperation
jokes.punchline: His dramatic plea for death
jokes.explanation: Classic George melodrama - his tendency to catastrophize minor inconveniences

jokes.text: What percentage of people would you say are good-looking? 25 percent. 25 percent? No way. It's, like, four to six percent. It's a 20-to-1 shot.
jokes.setup: Discussion of attractive people percentage
jokes.punchline: Jerry's ridiculously specific and pessimistic statistics
jokes.explanation: Classic Jerry observational comedy with absurdly specific numbers that feel researched

jokes.text: Have you been to the motor vehicle bureau? It's a leper colony there.
jokes.setup: Supporting his low attractiveness statistics
jokes.punchline: The DMV as evidence of universal unattractiveness
jokes.explanation: Strong observational comedy - everyone can relate to the DMV experience

jokes.text: Then how are all these people getting together? Alcohol.
jokes.setup: George's logical question about dating despite unattractiveness
jokes.punchline: Jerry's blunt, cynical answer
jokes.explanation: Perfect economic punchline that's both cynical and probably true

jokes.text: We have to have sex to save the friendship. Sex to save the friendship.
jokes.setup: Elaine's pragmatic approach to their relationship
jokes.punchline: The absurd inversion of typical relationship logic
jokes.explanation: Brilliant inversion of relationship tropes - sex as friendship preservation rather than complication

jokes.text: Eight dollars? Eight dollars? What are you doing? You're robbing me? I wasted my whole morning on you for $8?
jokes.setup: George's disappointment with prostitute payment
jokes.punchline: His indignant reaction to the low amount
jokes.explanation: George's cheapskate nature extends even to this situation - character-consistent but fairly predictable

jokes.text: All right, how often do we do it? Kramer, how is that important? Do you really think he's gonna ask that? Elaine, he's a psychiatrist. They're interested in stuff like that.
jokes.setup: Kramer wanting specific details for their fake relationship
jokes.punchline: His logical explanation about psychiatrist interests
jokes.explanation: Kramer's unexpected practical wisdom about professional interests - surprisingly logical for him

jokes.text: All right, all right. We do it five times a week. Oh, baby. Oh, man.
jokes.setup: Elaine giving specific frequency details
jokes.punchline: Kramer's inappropriate sexual excitement
jokes.explanation: Kramer's predictable horndog reaction - character-appropriate but not particularly clever

jokes.text: You know. Pleasuring you. It feels like aliens poking at my body.
jokes.setup: George's attempt at sexual prowess
jokes.punchline: Susan's brutal and bizarre description
jokes.explanation: Wonderfully specific and crushing metaphor for bad sex - the alien comparison is oddly perfect

jokes.text: So you think you're spongeworthy? Yes, I think I'm spongeworthy. I think I'm very spongeworthy.
jokes.setup: Elaine's contraceptive worthiness test
jokes.punchline: The absurd neologism and his earnest response
jokes.explanation: Iconic callback and neologism that entered popular culture - perfectly captures Elaine's practical approach to dating

jokes.text: Because if you don't get that return... that's a pretty big matzo ball hanging out there.
jokes.setup: Discussion of saying 'I love you' without reciprocation
jokes.punchline: The matzo ball metaphor for emotional vulnerability
jokes.explanation: Brilliant cultural metaphor that perfectly captures the weight of unreciprocated love declarations

jokes.text: What, you've never said it? Once, to a dog. He licked himself and left the room.
jokes.setup: George asking about Jerry's romantic history
jokes.punchline: Jerry's pathetic confession about declaring love to a dog
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation from awkward to absurd - the dog's reaction makes it even funnier

jokes.text: Let me ask you this. Is there any Tampax in your house? Yeah. Well, I'll tell you what you got here. You got yourself a girlfriend.
jokes.setup: Kramer's relationship diagnostic questions
jokes.punchline: The definitive Tampax test for girlfriend status
jokes.explanation: Brilliant relationship litmus test - crude but undeniably accurate diagnostic

jokes.text: Listen to this. Marcy comes over and she tells me... that her ex-boyfriend was over late last night... and, 'yada, yada, yada, I'm really tired today.' What do you think she was tired from? Well, obviously the yada, yada.
jokes.setup: George's story about Marcy's suspicious exhaustion
jokes.punchline: Jerry identifying the euphemistic yada yada
jokes.explanation: Great callback to the yada yada concept - Jerry's immediate understanding shows how universal the euphemism became

jokes.text: But you yada, yada'd over the best part. No, I mentioned the bisque.
jokes.setup: Jerry complaining about Elaine skipping the good part
jokes.punchline: Elaine's misunderstanding of what the 'best part' was
jokes.explanation: Perfect misdirection - Elaine prioritizing the meal over the sex reveals her character priorities

jokes.text: The only sex you're gonna have better than make-up sex... is if you're sent to prison and you have a conjugal visit. Yeah, conjugal visit sex. That is happening.
jokes.setup: Discussion of make-up sex quality
jokes.punchline: The absurd prison conjugal visit comparison
jokes.explanation: Dark comedy escalation that goes to unexpected territory - Kramer's enthusiasm sells it

jokes.text: You see, right now, I have relationship George. But there is also independent George. That's the George you know. The George you grew up with. Movie George. Coffee shop George. Liar George. Bawdy George.
jokes.setup: George explaining his dual identity crisis
jokes.punchline: The catalog of different Georges
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character analysis - perfectly captures the different personas people maintain in different contexts

jokes.text: A George divided against itself cannot stand!
jokes.setup: George's identity crisis reaching dramatic peak
jokes.punchline: The Lincoln House Divided speech applied to his dating life
jokes.explanation: Perfect historical reference applied to mundane personal crisis - captures George's tendency toward melodrama

jokes.text: She had man hands.
jokes.setup: Jerry explaining why he stopped dating someone
jokes.punchline: The simple, devastating physical description
jokes.explanation: Iconic callback line - perfectly economical description that became a cultural reference

jokes.text: She just dislikes me so much. It's irresistible.
jokes.setup: Jerry describing attraction to someone who hates him
jokes.punchline: Finding hatred irresistible
jokes.explanation: Perfectly captures Jerry's perverse dating psychology - attraction to unattainable/hostile partners

jokes.text: Let me understand. You got the hen, the chicken and the rooster. The rooster goes with the chicken. So who's having sex with the hen?
jokes.setup: Mr. Ross trying to understand poultry relationships
jokes.punchline: His confused logic about chicken sexuality
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist logic - Mr. Ross's earnest confusion about basic biology is perfectly in character

jokes.text: That's perverse.
jokes.setup: Learning that roosters mate with all the hens
jokes.punchline: His moral judgment of natural poultry behavior
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan reaction - applying human moral standards to animal behavior

jokes.text: Why don't you just tell me the name of the movie you selected?
jokes.setup: Jerry frustrated with automated movie phone system
jokes.punchline: His reasonable request that cuts through the technological nonsense
jokes.explanation: Perfect observational comedy about early automated systems - everyone's had this frustration

jokes.text: No soup for you! Come back, one year.
jokes.setup: Customer interaction with the Soup Nazi
jokes.punchline: The iconic rejection and banishment
jokes.explanation: Legendary catchphrase that became part of popular culture - perfect character moment and delivery

jokes.text: I don't even really work here. That's what makes this so difficult.
jokes.setup: Kramer being fired from job he doesn't actually have
jokes.punchline: Boss acknowledging the paradox makes it harder
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kramer absurdism - the boss's acknowledgment of the impossibility makes it funnier

jokes.text: Why do they call it Ovaltine? The mug is round. The jar is round. They should call it 'Roundtine.' That's gold, Jerry. Gold.
jokes.setup: Kenny's observation about Ovaltine naming logic
jokes.punchline: Kramer's enthusiastic validation of the obvious joke
jokes.explanation: Meta-joke about hacky observational comedy - Kramer's 'That's gold!' became its own catchphrase

jokes.text: If a woman can take your presence... for more than 10 seconds, you should hang onto her like grim death. Which is not far off, by the way.
jokes.setup: Uncle Leo giving dating advice
jokes.punchline: The morbid but practical relationship wisdom
jokes.explanation: Perfect Uncle Leo wisdom - combining dating advice with mortality awareness

jokes.text: Perhaps there's more to Newman than meets the eye. No, there's less.
jokes.setup: Suggestion that Newman has hidden depths
jokes.punchline: Jerry's immediate and definitive rejection
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jerry-Newman dynamic - the immediate dismissal with economic precision is classic

jokes.text: Oh, the humanity!
jokes.setup: Kramer witnessing some disaster
jokes.punchline: The classic Hindenburg disaster commentary
jokes.explanation: Classic historical reference - predictable but fits Kramer's dramatic tendencies

jokes.text: So you're still master of your domain? Yes. Yes, I am.
jokes.setup: Jerry checking on George's contest status
jokes.punchline: George's proud confirmation of self-control
jokes.explanation: Iconic callback to one of the most famous episodes - euphemism became part of cultural vocabulary

jokes.text: He took it out.
jokes.setup: Elaine's shocking revelation about her date
jokes.punchline: The infamous euphemistic accusation
jokes.explanation: Another legendary euphemistic callback - the economy and implication made it instantly iconic

jokes.text: They just write it off. Write it off what? Jerry, all these big companies, they write off everything. You don't even know what a write-off is. Do you? No, I don't. But they do. And they're the ones writing it off.
jokes.setup: Discussion of corporate tax write-offs
jokes.punchline: Kramer's circular logic about not understanding but trusting the system
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kramer logic - admits ignorance while maintaining complete confidence in the system
